suggestions.title: Dead code: activityLogLenRef is set but never read
suggestions.description: Lines 745–747 of App.jsx: `const activityLogLenRef = useRef(0)` is created and written to in a useEffect but is NEVER READ anywhere in the codebase. It's pure dead code that adds a side-effect run on every activity log change for zero benefit. Delete it.

suggestions.title: Pointless useMemo: stableActivityLog provides zero memoization
suggestions.description: `const stableActivityLog = useMemo(() => activityLog, [activityLog])` is functionally identical to `activityLog` itself. The dep array invalidates the memo the same moment the value changes — it will always return a fresh reference whenever `activityLog` changes. This misleads maintainers into thinking there's optimization happening when there is none. Just use `activityLog` directly everywhere stableActivityLog is referenced.

suggestions.title: stat-card--stuck is completely keyboard-inaccessible
suggestions.description: The 'Stuck Workers' stat card has an onClick handler that scrolls to a stuck worker, but has no `tabIndex`, no `role="button"`, and no `onKeyDown` handler. Keyboard-only users cannot activate it at all. Fix: add `tabIndex={0}`, `role="button"`, and `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { /* scroll logic */ } }}`.

suggestions.title: IIFEs inside JSX are a React anti-pattern (multiple instances in WorkerCard)
suggestions.description: WorkerCard uses `{(() => { const tags = getTaskTags(...); return tags.length > 0 ? ... : null })()}` and `{worker.status === 'working' && worker.updated_at && (() => { ... })()}` inline. IIFEs inside JSX create a new function object on every render, hurt readability, and signal missing component extraction. Extract these to named variables computed before the return, or to dedicated sub-components. There are at least 3 of these in WorkerCard alone.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime logic duplicated verbatim in Office.jsx ghost bubble handler
suggestions.description: App.jsx defines `getRelativeTime(timestamp)` at module scope (line 28). Office.jsx's ghost bubble IIFE (lines 524–530) duplicates the exact same logic inline. The function is never exported/imported between files. Either export `getRelativeTime` from App.jsx (or a utils file) and import it in Office.jsx, or move it to `src/utils/time.js`.

suggestions.title: Double ambient-particles layer: 19 animated elements running simultaneously
suggestions.description: App.jsx renders 12 `.ambient-particle` spans in a full-screen fixed div. Office.jsx renders another 7 ambient particles inside the office floor. That's 19 concurrently animated particles in addition to character bobs, clock animations, wander paths, progress rings, etc. The Office.jsx particles are redundant — the floor already has the full-app particles visible through it. Remove the second batch in Office.jsx.

suggestions.title: Activity log filter chain runs on every App render — should be memoized
suggestions.description: The expression `activityLog.filter(e => { ... }).filter(e => { ... })` in the Dashboard JSX runs on every render cycle, including the 60-second timestamp tick from `useTimeTick`. With large activity logs this is wasteful. Wrap with `useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(...).filter(...), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter])`.

suggestions.title: useTimeTick forces full App re-render every 60 seconds
suggestions.description: `useTimeTick(60_000)` is called at the top of App(), which means the ENTIRE component tree re-renders every minute just to refresh relative timestamps. The only consumers are activity log entries. Move the tick to a lower-level component (e.g., ActivityLog) or use a context so only timestamp-displaying nodes re-render.

suggestions.title: document.querySelector in StatsBar — React anti-pattern
suggestions.description: Line 430: `document.querySelector([data-worker-name="${name}"])?.scrollIntoView(...)` directly manipulates the DOM from a React component. This bypasses React's ref system and is fragile. The WorkerCard already holds a `cardRef` — instead pass a scroll-to callback from App to both WorkerCard and StatsBar, so the card calls it on mount when `isFocused` is true (this pathway already exists and works, just add scroll support there).

suggestions.title: Mobile office view: Luna character overlaps whiteboard area
suggestions.description: On the mobile screenshot (375×812), Luna's character renders over the whiteboard/left wall area. The wander keyframes use fixed percentage values (`left: 15–71%, top: 73–87%`) but at 480px the floor has a `max-height: 320px`. The top percentages (73–87%) may be visually outside usable bounds when aspect-ratio is forced and min-height constrains the floor. Test the wander bounds at small sizes and add floor-height-aware clamping or reduce the top% range.

suggestions.title: aria-live timestamps are invisible to screen readers (title attribute)
suggestions.description: ActivityLogEntry shows timestamps with `title={formatFullDateTime(...)}` as the accessible label. The `title` attribute is not reliably announced by screen readers. Users navigating by keyboard also can't trigger the tooltip. Consider adding a visually-hidden `<time dateTime={...}>` element alongside the relative timestamp, or use an `aria-label` on the time span.

suggestions.title: Office.jsx is 1183 lines — needs splitting
suggestions.description: Office.jsx has grown to 1183 lines and contains CharacterAvatar (288 lines of SVG alone), Character, WallClock, WindowElement, ConferenceTable, AwaySign, VibeShockwave, and the main Office component. This violates single-responsibility and makes navigation painful. At minimum extract CharacterAvatar to `src/components/CharacterAvatar.jsx` and WallClock/WindowElement to `src/components/OfficeDecor.jsx`.

suggestions.title: Office characters have no keyboard affordance despite being interactive
suggestions.description: Working and idle characters have `pointer-events: auto` and click handlers, but no visible focus indicator and their `tabIndex={0}` is set without communicating what pressing Enter/Space will do. The CSS only provides `data-tooltip::after` for hover — there's no equivalent for focus state. Non-mouse users have no way to discover these characters are clickable.

suggestions.title: completionToast rendered with inline styles — CSS class needed
suggestions.description: The completion toast (line 932–956) uses a massive inline `style` object that's inconsistent with the rest of the codebase where everything uses CSS classes. This makes theming, dark/light mode support, and reduced-motion preferences impossible to apply to the toast. Extract to a `.completion-toast` CSS class in App.css.

suggestions.title: window.location.hash mutations are scattered and duplicated
suggestions.description: There are at least 6 places in App.jsx that do `setTab('X'); window.location.hash = 'X'` as a paired operation. Extract a `navigateToTab(tab)` helper that does both atomically, reducing duplication and making it easier to add tab-change side effects later.

suggestions.title: No error boundary — uncaught throws crash the whole app
suggestions.description: There's no React error boundary wrapping the main content. If CharacterAvatar throws (e.g., a malformed SVG prop), Office, Dashboard, or any component, the user sees a blank white screen with no way to recover. Add a simple ErrorBoundary component wrapping the `<main>` that shows a 'Something went wrong — ↺ Retry' message.

suggestions.title: Vibe sparkline circle tooltips use SVG <title> — poor SR support
suggestions.description: The VibeSparkline circles use `<title>` child elements for tooltip text. SVG `<title>` has inconsistent screen reader support across browsers. Use `aria-label` on each circle (which already exist as interactive SVG elements) or wrap in a `<g role="img" aria-label="...">` instead.

suggestions.title: ETA calculation likely always null — startedAt field name mismatch
suggestions.description: ProgressBar receives `startedAt={worker.startedAt}` and uses it for ETA. Confirmed: workers.json DOES use `startedAt` (camelCase), so the field is correctly named. However, the ETA condition `progress > 5 && progress < 100 && workingMins > 1` means ETA only shows when workers have been going more than 1 minute at >5% progress. Verify the ETA actually appears in practice — it may never show in the UI due to the conditions.
